title: 第 51 節
en: VII PRAPATOAKA, II KHANDA, I. 117 food, he is able to see, hear, perceive, think, act, and understand. Meditate on food.
2. ' He who meditates on food as Brahman, obtains the worlds rich in food and drink ; he is, as it were, lord and master as far as food reacheshe who meditates on food as Brahman.'
' Sir, is there something better than food ? '
' Yes, there is something better than food.'
' Sir, tell it me/ Tenth Khanda.

1. 'Water (ap) is better than food. Therefore if there is not sufficient rain, the vital spirits fail from fear that there will be less food. But if there is sufficient rain, the vital spirits rejoice, because there will be much food. This water, on assuming different forms, becomes this earth, this sky, this heaven, the mountains, gods and men, cattle, birds, herbs and trees, all beasts down to worms, midges, and ants. Water indeed assumes all these forms. Meditate on water.
2. 'He who meditates on water as Brahman, obtains all wishes, he becomes satisfied ; he is, as it were, lord and master as far as water reaches — he who meditates on water as Brahman.'
' Sir, is there something better than water ? ' 1 Yes, there is something better than water.' ' Sir, tell it me.'
Eleventh Khanda.

1. 'Fire (te^as) is better than water. For fire united with air, warms the ether. Then people say, It is hot, it burns, it will rain. Thus does fire, after showing this sign (itself) first, create water. And I I 8 Z77ANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.

thus again thunderclaps come with lightnings, flashing upwards and across the sky. Then people say, There is lightning and thunder, it will rain. Then also does fire, after showing this sign first, create water. Meditate on fire.

2. ' He who meditates on fire as Brahman, obtains, resplendent himself, resplendent worlds, full of light and free of darkness ; he is, as it were, lord and master as far as fire reaches — he who meditates on fire as Brahman.'
' Sir, is there something better than fire ? '
' Yes, there is something better than fire.'
' Sir, tell it me.'
Twelfth Khanda.

i. 'Ether (or space) is better than fire. For in the ether exist both sun and moon, the lightning, stars, and fire (agni). Through the ether we call, through the ether we hear, through the ether we answer1. In the ether or space we rejoice (when we are together), and rejoice not (when we are separated). In the ether everything is born, and towards the ether everything tends when it is born 2. Meditate on ether.

2. ' He who meditates on ether as Brahman, obtains the worlds of ether and of light, which are free from pressure and pain, wide and spacious3; he is, as it were, lord and master as far as ether reaches — he who meditates on ether as Brahman.'
' Sir, is there something better than ether ? '
1 Cf. Kh. Up. IV, 5, i.
2 The seed grows upwards towards the ether ; not downwards.
3 Cf. Km. Up. II, ii.
VII PRAPArtfAKA, 14 KHANDA, 2. II9 ' Yes, there is something better than ether.' ' Sir, tell it me.'
Thirteenth Kha^a.

1. 'Memory1 (smara) is better than ether. Therefore where many are assembled together, if they have no memory, they would hear no one, they would not perceive, they would not understand. Through memory we know our sons, through memory our cattle. Meditate on memory.
2. ' He who meditates on memory as Brahman, is, as it were, lord and master as far as memory reaches — he who meditates on memory as Brahman.'
' Sir, is there something better than memory ? ' ' Yes, there is something better than memory.' 1 Sir, tell it me.'
Fourteenth Khanda.
1. ' Hope (asa) is better than memory. Fired by hope does memory read the sacred hymns, perform sacrifices, desire sons and cattle, desire this world and the other. Meditate on hope.

2. ' He who meditates on hope as Brahman, all his desires are fulfilled by hope, his prayers are not in vain ; he is, as it were, lord and master as far as hope reaches — he who meditates on hope as Brahman.'
zh: VII PRAPATOAKA, II KHANDA, I. 117 食物，他能夠看見、聽見、感知、思考、行動與理解。當靜思食物。
2. 「冥想食物即梵天者，獲得充滿飲食之世界；他實為食物所及之處的主宰與統治者——冥想食物即梵天者。」
「先生，有比食物更好的東西嗎？」
「是的，有比食物更好的東西。」
閣下，請為我說述之。 第十分篇。

'Water (ap) is better than food. Therefore if there is not sufficient rain, the vital spirits fail from fear that there will be less food. But if there is sufficient rain, the vital spirits rejoice, because there will be much food. This water, on assuming different forms, becomes this earth, this sky, this heaven, the mountains, gods and men, cattle, birds, herbs and trees, all beasts down to worms, midges, and ants. Water indeed assumes all these forms. Meditate on water. 「水（ap）優於食物。因此，若雨量不足，生命精靈便因恐懼糧食減少而衰竭。但若雨量充沛，生命精靈則因糧食豐富而欣喜。此水，在承受不同形態時，化為此大地、此蒼穹、此天界、諸山、諸神與人類、牲畜、飛鳥、草本與樹木、所有禽獸直至蟲、蠓及螞蟻。水確實承受一切此等形態。當冥想於水。」
2. 「凡冥想水為梵天者，獲得一切所願，得以滿足；他儼然成為水所及之處的主宰與統治者——凡冥想水為梵天者。」
'先生，有比水更好的東西嗎？'¹ 是的，有比水更好的東西。' '先生，請告訴我。'
第十一則（Khanda）
「火（te^as）優於水。因為火與空氣結合，溫暖以太。然後人們說，『它很熱，它燃燒，它會下雨。』因此火在首先展現這個徵兆（它本身）之後，創造水。」 ——《詹多基亞奧義書》（CHANDOGYA-UPANISHAD）
因此雷鳴伴隨著閃電而來，在天空中上下左右地閃爍。那時人們說：「有閃電和雷鳴，將要下雨。」於是火焰在首先顯示這個徵兆之後，就創造了水。你當冥想火。
2. 「那冥思梵天為火者，其身遍照，獲得光明遍滿、黑暗淨除之世界；彼如是者，於火所及之處，為主為尊——冥思梵天為火者。」
「先生，有什麼東西比火更好嗎？」
「是的，有比火更好的東西。」
「先生，請告訴我。」
第十二篇章
i. 「以太（或空間）優於火。蓋以太中既存日月、閃電、星辰及火（agni）。我們藉以太而呼喚，藉以太而聽聞，藉以太而應答¹。在以太或空間中，我們相聚則喜樂，相離則不喜樂。萬物於以太中誕生，其既誕生則趨向以太²。應冥想以太。
2. 「凡冥想以太為梵天者，獲得以太與光明之世界，此等世界超越壓力與痛苦，廣闊無垠；彼如同以太所及之處的主宰與統治者——凡冥想以太為梵天者。」³
「先生，有什麼東西比乙醚更優良嗎？」
參考《歌者奧義書》（Kh. Up.）第四卷第五篇第一節。
種子向上朝向乙太生長；而非向下。
3 參見《卡塔奧義書》II, ii。
VII 普拉帕塔卡 14 坎達 2。119 「是的，有某物優於以太。」「先生，請告訴我。」
第十三卡樂章
「記憶（smara）優於乙太。因此當許多人聚集一處，倘若他們沒有記憶，他們將聽不到任何人，將無法感知，將無法理解。藉由記憶我們認識我們的兒子，藉由記憶我們認識我們的牲畜。當思念記憶吧。
2. 「誰若冥想記憶為梵（Brahman），則彷彿在記憶所及之處成為主宰與統治者——誰若冥想記憶為梵。」
「先生，有沒有什麼東西比記憶更好的呢？」「有的，確實有比記憶更好的東西。」¹「先生，請告訴我吧。」
第十四章（Khanda）
希望（asa）勝於記憶。受希望之火激勵，記憶誦讀神聖的讚美詩，進行祭祀，渴望兒子與牲畜，渴望此世與來世。當思念希望。
2. 「誰若思想希望為梵天，則其一切願望均得希望所滿足，其祈禱亦非徒勞；彼人乃若為主君與統治者，其範圍至希望所及之處——誰若思想希望為梵天者。」
